title: Clarified PRD analysis
description: Structured, build-ready output of the figma-product-prd PM quality gate.

targetPlatform.description: The platform(s) this product targets. First-class field — must be USER-CONFIRMED; 'confirmed:false' blocks Step 1 close.

platforms.description: One or more of the canonical platform values. 'responsive-web' means the product must satisfy both mobile and desktop conventions at their respective widths.

confirmed.description: Must be true before Step 1 can close. A PRD that does not state platform makes this [NEEDS INPUT], not [AUTO].

deviceFrameDefaults.description: Default frame dimensions for each platform in this list. Drives the builder's canonical device width.

safeAreaNeeded.description: True for mobile-native-ios / mobile-native-android / mobile-web. Safe-area insets (notch, home indicator) must be respected in the layout.

audience.description: Who this product is for and how they think — derived from the PRD (product + actors + REFERENCE docs), not invented. It is what Step 2 (Direction) grounds its art direction on. See references/audience-research.md.

primaryPersona.description: 1–2 primary personas (role + who they actually are). Not a gallery.

ageContext.description: Age band / locale / tech-savviness and the usage context (device, setting, session length, stress level) — only what changes a design decision.

mentalModel.description: The metaphors/expectations the user already holds (which apps have trained them; what they expect a 'transfer'/'dashboard'/'feed' to feel like).

accessibilityNeeds.description: Contrast/text-size sensitivity, reduced-motion, one-handed reach, color-blind-safe status signaling — any the audience plausibly needs.

jobsToBeDone.description: Top 3 outcomes the user is hiring this product for, phrased as outcomes not features.

screen.description: screen id this step lands on

purpose.description: One line: what this screen is FOR — its job/intent. Defended in the exit-review; it is what Step 2 Direction grounds the screen's treatment on.

register.description: INITIAL register tag (the creativity dial). This is the source of truth ONLY for the initial pass — Step 2 concept.md's 'Register map' FINALIZES it, and Steps 3–5 only reference the finalized value, never re-decide it. energy = expressive/competitive/hero; trust = transactional/calm/legibility-first; neutral = utility/scanning.

effect.description: what it does / where it goes

successMetrics.description: What the PRD says success looks like — the outcomes the design must make achievable (e.g. 'user knows their balance is safe in 2s', a stated conversion/completion target). Derived from the PRD; if the PRD is silent, surface it as an openQuestion rather than inventing a metric. Required field, but an empty array is valid when the user confirms there is no formal success target.

constraints.description: Hard constraints the PRD imposes — platform/compliance/performance/legal/brand. Distinct from successMetrics (those are goals; these are non-negotiable limits the build must respect).

openQuestions.description: Every functional gap surfaced. Step 1 closes only when none are unresolved.

screen.description: screen id, or null for cross-cutting

resolution.description: the user's answer or the recorded inference
